title: Data Annotation Lead
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
We're looking for a Data Annotations Lead to join our Data team and own the people side of data annotation.
This role is about building and leading a world class in-house data annotation team that is able to pivot quickly to any research experiment while delivering on quality, quantity, and variety when it comes to data.
You'll work in coordination with Machine Learning, Software engineering, and Data to define the framework and tools on which to build a data annotation team around. Your role is vital to ensuring our data annotators are aligned with the guidances for annotations and are upholding a culture of happiness.
What You'll Do
• Build a data annotation team
• Manage the people side of data annotations
• Create documentation
• Be in the weeds and annotate data yourself anytime something new is being designed
• Create data annotation processes
What You'll Bring
• Clear written and verbal communication to guide our data annotators
• Your ability to manage unexpected challenges
• Your ownership of key stakeholders with data annotators, engineering, and support
• Excitement for the growth and development of data annotation
• Someone adept at prioritization of competing requests, who's able to move both quickly, and in an organized manner
• A level of hardcore-ness while still treating people like people
• Intermediate level understanding of ML
Nice to Have
• Previous experience leading data annotation teams
• Technical skills to build tools for data annotation
• Ability to leverage AI to help improve productivity
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: Eval Ops Associate
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
We're looking for an Eval Ops Associate to join our Data team and support eval operations. This role is about keeping eval logistics tight so operations run without friction and translating eval data into insights to help the ML team make better decisions.
You'll work in coordination with Machine Learning, the Eval Operations Lead, and our in-house Memory Developers to understand what decision each eval is meant to inform, improve the quality and integrity of the data we collect, and make sure the physical environments and props we need are sourced, ready, and standardized. Your role is vital to ensuring evals produce data the ML team can actually make decisions on, by building the analytics, processes, and physical infrastructure that hold the operation together.
What You'll Do
• Build the data and reporting layer of eval ops – such as throughput, success/fail rates, incident trends – and distill insights to help the ML team make better decisions
• Coordinate the logistics that let us run evals at scale including sourcing and buying props, garments, and equipment for the physical environments, and keeping them standardized across sessions
• Scout and set up new spaces to expand eval capacity
• Create and enforce documentation and process
What You'll Bring
• Strong data analytics fluency
• High agency — you see a gap, scope it, and close it without waiting to be told, whether that's a broken dashboard or a prop run to the store
• Clear written and verbal communication to coordinate with ML and Memory Developers
• Someone adept at prioritizing competing requests, able to move quickly and in an organized manner
• Comfort with hands-on logistics — sourcing, purchasing, and physically setting up environments
• A level of hardcore-ness while still treating people like people
• Intermediate-level understanding of ML
Nice to Have
• Previous experience in ops, research-ops, or data-ops at a fast-moving company
• Technical skills to build lightweight tooling or automations
• Exposure to ML evaluation, experimentation, or labeling workflows
• Ability to leverage AI to improve productivity
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: Memory Developer (in-house)
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect  
As an in-house Memory Developer, you won't just collect data, you will shape the "memory" of our robot and play a vital role in training the AI that powers it. Using provided hardware, you will record high-quality demonstrations of tasks that directly influence how our robot learns to perceive and act in real environments. Additionally, you'll help test out new tasks, hardware, and software before we roll out to our remote team of data collectors. This is a flexible, on-site, role for individuals who are excited to contribute to the future of robotics.  
Compensation follows an hourly basis: 
• $35 per hour  
Contractors have the flexibility to work a fixed schedule of their choice of minimum 3 days and 5 days a week. Must work 8 hours per day.
There is also opportunity for growth in terms of learning additional skillsets.
What You'll Do  
• Follow detailed task instructions to ensure high-quality data 
• Record and submit video demonstrations of household tasks (e.g., folding t-shirts, arranging shoes, etc.)
• Pilot and provide feedback on new tasks 
• Test out new hardware and software 
• Apply feedback from reviewer 
• Communicate clearly and promptly 
What You'll Bring  
• Reliability and consistency in completing tasks 
• Willingness to learn, adapt, and incorporate feedback 
• Curiosity and excitement about personal robotics  
• Kinesthetic intelligence
• Strong communication
• Integrity
• Ability to embrace the mundane
• Solid work ethic
• Successfully complete a standard background check as part of onboarding
Benefits
• Free lunch and dinner provided
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: Electrical Engineer
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to ExpectYou will be one of the early members of the EE & Firmware team, with the opportunity to significantly influence the EE design across all aspects of our home robot.  This includes sensing, networking, compute and power distribution.
You will also collaborate closely with mechanical and software teams to integrate electrical systems into our wearables and robotic platforms, driving the hardware foundation of our mission to redefine home robotics.
What You'll Do• Architect, design, and build rigid and/or flex PCBAs (schematic capture, simulation, board layout)
• Own board and system bring-up
• Design and execute validation testing plans for boards and systems
• Debug and triage units from the field
• Create and modify testing software for these PCBAs
What You'll Bring• 2+ years of electrical engineering experience (this posting includes all levels)
• BS or MS degree in Electrical Engineering, related technical field, or equivalent practical experience
• PCB design experience - schematic capture, board layout, manufacturing, test/debug
• Delivered at least one project from prototype phase into production
• Experience with hardware validation cycles, debug and measurement methods
• Experience with lab bring-up, debug of electronic systems, root cause analysis, and manufacturing testing processes
• Experience with high speed design
• Experience with wire harnesses, cabling & connectors.
• Aptitude for quickly helping colleagues root cause EE issues on development robots
Nice to Have• Altium Designer
• Designed complex high-speed FlexPCBs
• Familiar with SI/PI simulation tools
• Network analyzers, TDR, spectrum analyzers
• Higher speed buses such as MIPI, GMSL, DDR, PCIe
• Designed motor controllers or other high power PCBs for electromechanical actuators/robotic systems and tight integration within them
• Compute architectures / design of mainboards, networking equipment, camera interfacing

title: Embedded Linux Software Engineer
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
As an Embedded Linux Software Engineer, you’ll own platform software for the embedded Linux compute units running our robotics stack. You will collaborate closely with electrical, mechanical, software, and machine-learning teams to integrate our full robotics stack on a new compute.
What You'll Do
• Own the embedded software stack (bootloader, kernel, devicetree, BSP, and drivers) for compute platforms based on SoCs like NVIDIA Jetson, Qualcomm etc
• Develop and maintain Linux kernel drivers and userspace integration for sensors, GMSL cameras, displays, storage, and high-speed interconnects (PCIe, USB, MIPI CSI/DSI, Ethernet, CAN)
• Own build system and BSP layers using Yocto/Buildroot or vendor SDKs like JetPack
• Work with the electrical engineering team to debug and bring up custom carrier boards
• Work closely with the software and machine-learning teams to optimize performance across the entire software stack
• Design and implement OTA end-to-end using RAUC, Mender, SWUpdate, or equivalent
What You'll Bring
• BS or MS in CS, Computer Engineering, EE, related technical field, or equivalent practical experience
• 3+ years of embedded Linux / systems software development, including hands-on bringup on modern application-class SoCs (NVIDIA Jetson, Qualcomm Snapdragon, or similar)
• Strong proficiency in C and ideally C++ and/or Rust
• Strong experience with Yocto, Buildroot, or an equivalent embedded Linux build system, including custom layers/recipes and production image customization
• Deep understanding of the Linux boot flow, from bootloader to userspace, and low-level peripherals
• Comfortable working alongside electrical engineers: reading schematics, driving scopes, logic analyzers, and JTAG, and debugging at the hardware/software boundary
• Knowledge of power management, secure boot, watchdogs, and other production-readiness concerns
• Experience developing CI pipelines for embedded Linux images
Nice to Have
• Delivered at least one project from prototype phase into production
• Experience with PREEMPT_RT
• Familiarity with RTOS-based firmware development
• Experience with Python for scripting, data analysis, or test automation
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: Firmware Engineer - Motor Control
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
You will be one of the early members of the EE & Firmware team, with the opportunity to own the motor control firmware that drives every actuator in our home robot. You'll architect and tune the low-level control systems that translate high-level commands into precise, reliable motion.
You will collaborate closely with electrical, mechanical, and machine-learning teams to integrate motor control firmware with custom hardware, enabling force-sensitive and compliant control behaviors across our robotic platforms.
What You'll Do
• Own motor control firmware end-to-end, from prototype to production, working closely with electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, and machine learning teams
• Design and implement motor control firmware on brushless actuators; implement and tune FOC, current, speed, and position control loops
• Bring up and debug custom motor control hardware and sensors; collaborate with hardware engineers on board bring-up
• Build robust sensing and calibration pipelines for encoders and current/voltage measurements
• Work closely with controls and machine learning engineers to enable force-sensitive, compliant control under dynamic loads
What You'll Bring
• BS or MS degree in Computer or Electrical Engineering, related technical field, or equivalent practical experience
• 3+ years of real-time embedded software development such as architecture design, microcontrollers, drivers & libraries
• Strong proficiency in embedded C/C++ with a deep understanding of real-time constraints and low-level peripherals (timers, ADCs, PWMs, DMA, non-volatile memory)
• Deep knowledge of motor control theory (field-oriented control, dq transforms), with hands-on experience
• Experience with encoders (of various types), and current sensors, including calibration, filtering, and synchronization for reliable control feedback
• Experience working with electrical engineering teams, with the ability to read and understand schematics and layout
• Comfortable bringing up new boards, debugging cross-functional issues, and working with Product team to design around real-world conditions
• Experience with communication protocols such as I2C, SPI, UART, RS-485, CAN (FD), Ethernet, EtherCAT
• Knowledge of safety mechanisms (overcurrent, overtemp, watchdogs, brownout protection) and how to implement them robustly
Nice to Have
• Delivered at least one project from prototype phase into production
• Familiarity with RTOS concepts and experience implementing them using industry-standard RTOS environments, including scheduling, interrupt handling, and real-time guarantees
• Experience with Python for scripting, data analysis, or test automation
• Cross-disciplinary skills such as system architecture design, protocol selection, or familiarity working with ML-driven control pipelines
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: Mechanical Engineer
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to ExpectWe are looking for a Mechanical Engineer to help design, build, and scale the mechanical systems that power our robots and data collection devices. You will play a key role in turning prototypes into reliable, manufacturable products, working hands-on with early designs and suppliers to ensure quality and scalability.
This role is ideal for an engineer excited about working across the full product lifecycle — from whiteboard sketches and 3D CAD to testing, validation, and manufacturing handoff.
What You’ll Do• Support the design and development of mechanical components and assemblies for wearable and robotic devices
• Rapidly prototype, test, and iterate on designs to improve performance and reliability
• Contribute to DFM/DFA efforts to ensure designs can scale from low-volume prototypes to high-volume production
• Work with vendors and suppliers on prototyping, tooling, and production readiness
• Perform basic analyses (tolerance, structural, thermal) to validate design decisions
• Assist in documentation for design reviews, testing, and production release
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams (electrical, software, industrial design) to integrate mechanical systems
What You’ll Bring• 2+ years of mechanical engineering experience (this post includes all levels)
• Strong skills in CAD (OnShape or SolidWorks preferred) and a familiarity with rapid prototyping techniques (3D printing, machining, etc.)
• Understanding of DFM/DFA principles and interest in scaling prototypes to production
• Experience working on consumer hardware, robotics, or compact devices is a plus
• Hands-on problem-solving approach with ability to iterate quickly
• Strong collaboration skills and comfort working in fast-paced startup environments
• Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering or related field (Master’s a plus, but not required)

title: ML Infrastructure Engineer
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
The Role
Sunday Robotics is building the future of home robotics. We're developing end-to-end ML models for robot manipulation, and you'll have the opportunity to build and shape foundational systems that directly accelerate our path to putting robots in homes.
This is a broad role that can be tailored to your specific area of expertise: data pipelines, training infrastructure or inference. You'll build systems across the full robot learning pipeline: ingesting and processing multimodal data, scaling distributed training, optimizing inference for real-time control and building research tooling.
What You'll Do
Training and Inference Infrastructure
• Maintain an effective research codebase with good ergonomics, optimizing for fast iteration and correctness
• Own infrastructure for model training: job scheduling, checkpointing, metrics, and logging
• Scale distributed training across GPU clusters with minimal researcher friction
• Enable training of larger models through sharding, activation checkpointing and memory optimization
• Profile and optimize gpu utilization, memory usage and training throughput
• Build low-latency inference pipeline for real-time robot control, apply quantization, distillation and model compilation to optimize inference performance
• Work closely with researchers and roboticists to translate research needs into reliable software and infrastructure
Data Pipelines and Research Tooling
• Design high-throughput pipelines for ingesting, validating, and transforming multimodal robot data (video, proprioception, actions)
• Build storage systems and metadata indexing for efficient dataset management at large scale
• Optimize dataloaders, sharding and prefetching to minimize time from data arrival to model training
• Build research tooling for debugging, visualization and experiment analysis
What We're Looking For
• Strong software engineering and systems fundamentals
• Experience building distributed systems or large-scale data pipelines
• Hands-on experience with ML training infrastructure, ideally PyTorch
• Comfort reasoning about performance, memory, I/O, and GPU utilization
• Experience managing training workloads (SLURM, Kubernetes, or similar)
• Ownership mindset: you design, build, operate, and iterate on systems end-to-end
• Enjoy working closely with researchers and unblocking fast-moving projects
Nice to Have
• Experience with robotics data pipelines or multimodal models
• Background in VLAs, Video Generation architectures or robot learning systems
• Deep ML systems experience: training compilers, custom kernels, runtime optimization
• Hands-on GPU performance tuning
• Experience with serialization formats for high-performance systems (Protobuf, FlatBuffers, MCAP)
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: Production Support Associate
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
We are seeking a proactive and versatile Production Support Associate (1-year contract) to join our manufacturing team. In this role, you will serve as a critical link in our production lifecycle, ensuring operational continuity through precise materials kitting, inventory management, and floor optimization. This is a 1-year contract position with the potential to transition into a full-time role based on performance and business needs.
What You'll Do
• Additive Manufacturing Operations: Manage the full lifecycle of a 3D printer fleet; including part extraction, build plate restoration, and hardware resets to maintain a 24/7 production cadence.
• Post-Processing: Perform precision cleaning and surface finishing on robotic components to prepare them for integration.
• Hardware Quality Control (QC): Execute rigorous incoming and post-production inspections. You will be the "first and last line of defense" to ensure parts and assemblies meet the standards.
• Strategic Kitting & Inventory: Orchestrate the organization of assembly kits; ensuring mechanical engineers and technicians have the exact sub-assemblies needed for rapid iterations.
• Equipment Lifecycle & Maintenance: Conduct routine preventative maintenance on manufacturing equipment to eliminate unplanned downtime.
• Inventory Control: Monitor stock levels of critical parts and consumables.
What You'll Bring
• Rapid Technical Adaptation: Proven ability to quickly master new technologies, software interfaces, and evolving production workflows in a fast-paced environment.
• Attention to Detail: Ability to spot minor defects during quality inspections and follow complex SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures).
• Mechanical Skills: Comfortable using basic hand tools for machine maintenance and part post-processing.
• Physical Stamina: Ability to stand for extended periods and handle materials, including lifting and moving production components.
• Organizational Skills: Strong ability to track inventory, manage "kitting" lists, and multitask in a fast-paced environment.
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. title: Community Lead
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
As our Community Lead, you will be the bridge between Sunday and the world—both in digital spaces and in real life. Your mission is to cultivate a passionate ecosystem of early adopters, developers, potential hires, and future robot owners.
You will be responsible for bringing the Sunday brand to life through IRL experiences, from intimate hiring dinners to eventual large-scale pop-ups and partnerships involving Memo. Simultaneously, you’ll lead our digital community, sparking conversations and cultivating a consistent tone for our major social channels (X, IG, YT, TT). You’ll work closely with our Creative Director and Head of Marketing to align our online community strategy with our broader brand narrative.
What You’ll Do
• IRL Community Building: Design and execute high-touch events including social mixers, industry hiring dinners, and immersive pop-up experiences that showcase our Memo in the real world (longer term).
• Digital Community Management: Own the day-to-day engagement across X, Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn. You’ll foster a genuine and consistent sense of belonging among our followers.
• Network Growth: Identify and nurture relationships with robotics enthusiasts, tech community leaders, and the general public to expand Sunday’s footprint.
• Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with our Creative Director to ensure our physical touchpoints (events/merch/pop-ups) are world-class.
What You’ll Bring
• Experience: 3+ years of community management experience at a high-paced startup or high-growth tech company.
• Event Track Record: A proven history of producing successful IRL events—you should have a portfolio or specific examples of events you’ve owned from concept to execution.
• Social Fluency: Strong experience with digital community management and a deep understanding of how to drive engagement on modern social platforms.
• Interpersonal Mastery: You are a "super-connector" comfortable hosting everything from a dinner for 10 engineers to community events for hundreds of newcomers.
• Operational Excellence: You are highly organized and capable of managing event logistics, budgets, and vendor relationships.
Nice to Have
• Zero-to-One Experience: Experience building a community from scratch for a new product or brand.
• Tech Enthusiasm: A passion for robotics, AI, or "hard tech" and an ability to translate complex technical concepts into community-driven excitement.
• Content Skills: Ability to capture "lo-fi" social content at events to capture moments that can be shared across social media.
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: Creative Director
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
As our Creative Director, you’ll steward our brand’s look and feel across video production,  brand standards, and novel marketing deployments of Memo.
You’ll manage vendors/contractors to deliver video campaigns documenting the launch and experience of our Beta, enforce consistent creative brand and tone across our social channels, and manage our visual design identity.
Longterm, you will work closely with the Head of Marketing in executing new concepts, such as showrooms and other real-life robot deployments. This entails both high-level strategic concepting, as well as fast-paced execution.
It will be your responsibility to ensure launch moments and research breakthroughs are documented and shared with the world. This includes close collaboration with our Social Media lead for brand consistency and distribution across channels.
What You’ll Do
Creative Direction
• Own the visual identity and creative direction across our website, company merch, and photography assets. Robot and companion app design managed separately from this role
• Develop concept-to-delivery creative for product films, launch videos, demo reels, and motion assets
• Collaborate across marketing, product, design to define tone, narrative, and visual strategy for major moments
Motion & Video
• Lead creative for video content: product demos, announcements, behind-the-scenes, and feature spotlights
• Oversee storyboarding, scripting, filming, directing, and editing
• Guide motion graphics and animation that showcase robot capabilities and product experiences
Design & Brand Identity 
• Manage media kit requests, our Design Language System assets, and campaign-specific visuals + iconography
• Design physical touchpoints such as robot beta program welcome kits, packaging, and printed materials 
• Ensure design consistency and quality across all internal and external outputs
Cross-Functional Collaboration and Management
• Partner closely with engineering / product to translate technical features into clear, intuitive assets
• Manage creative vendors as needed (production crews, animators, designers)
What You’ll Bring
• 4+ years of experience in creative direction, visual design, or film/video at a high-growth startup, agency, or product-focused company
• A world-class portfolio showcasing brand, video, motion, and design work
• Strong creative vision paired with the ability to execute quickly and independently
• Deep expertise in video production (storyboarding, cinematography, directing, editing, sound)
• Strong design skills: graphic design, layout, typography, color, and brand systems
• Ability to create content that blends technical clarity with emotional resonance
• A high bar for craft, detail, and polish — with a willingness to iterate rapidly
Nice to Have
• Passion for robotics, emerging technology, or storytelling around complex products
• Experience building or evolving a brand from zero-to-one
• Motion design and animation chops (After Effects, Blender, etc.)
• Experience designing physical or packaging experiences
• Familiarity with robotics, hardware, AI, or industrial design
• Ability to direct photoshoots or run a small in-house production environment
• Experience managing or mentoring other creatives
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

title: Developer Infrastructure Engineer
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
The ML & Robotics Infra team builds the foundational systems that every part of our robot perception, ML, controls and behavior runs on, and the developer infrastructure that lets us build, ship, and update that software quickly and safely on every robot in the fleet.
As a Developer Infrastructure Engineer, you’ll own how code and model artifacts get built and delivered to the robot. From the moment a developer hits commit to the moment the new binary, middleware, or model is running on a robot in someone’s home. You’ll build the systems that let our engineers ship quickly and confidently: a fast and reliable build, a CI/CD pipeline they trust, signed and reproducible artifacts and an OTA path that gets those artifacts safely onto the fleet. You’ll work alongside teammates who own the runtime substrate and the GPU and accelerated compute layer, they build what runs on the robot; you build how it gets there.
What You’ll Do
• Build system: Own and evolve our build system to keep builds fast, hermetic, and reliable as the codebase and team grow
• CI/CD: Design and operate pre-merge and post-merge pipelines that catch regressions early and produce trustworthy release artifacts
• Artifact pipeline: Build the packaging, versioning, and signing pipeline for the binaries that ship to the robot i.e. system software, application code, and exported/compiled ML models
• Dev containers & dependency management: Define and maintain consistent development containers and package/dependency management across the team, keeping the dev environment in parity with the base Linux image that runs on the robot
• Over-the-air updates: Build and operate the OTA system that delivers new software to robots safely — staged rollouts, canarying, rollback, and verification
• Model delivery: Treat compiled/exported models as first-class deployable artifacts, with versioning, validation, and the same delivery guarantees as the rest of the stack
• Developer experience: Reduce the time between “I have an idea” and “it’s running on a robot” for local dev workflows, fast feedback loops, and clear failure signals when things break
What You’ll Bring
• 5+ years of software engineering experience, with significant time spent on build infrastructure, developer tooling, CI/CD, or software delivery
• Hands-on experience with a modern build system (Bazel, Buck, CMake at scale, or similar) in a large multi-language codebase
• Strong CI/CD background — designing pipelines, managing test infrastructure, and improving signal-to-noise as systems grow
• Working knowledge of containers (Docker/OCI) and the practical tradeoffs around image building, layering, and runtime
• Experience deploying software to production environments — whether cloud, embedded devices, fleets, or similar — with a real understanding of versioning, rollback, and safe rollout
• Comfort working in a systems language (C++, Rust, or Go) and a scripting language (Python) — you’ll read and write both
• Working comfort on Linux as a target deployment platform
Nice to Have• Direct experience with Bazel in a polyglot codebase (C++, Python, ML model toolchains)
• Experience building or operating OTA update systems for embedded devices, vehicles, robots, or similar fleets
• Experience packaging and deploying ML models — model registries, versioning, compiled/exported artifact pipelines
• Familiarity with artifact signing, supply chain security, or reproducible builds
• Background working alongside runtime, robotics or ML teams
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: System Software Engineer — GPU & Accelerated Compute
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
The ML & Robotics Infra team builds the foundational systems that every part of our robot perception, ML, controls and behavior runs on, and the developer infrastructure that lets us build, ship, and update that software quickly and safely on every robot in the fleet.
As a System Software Engineer on ML & Robotics Infra focused on GPU and accelerated compute, you’ll own how every accelerated workload on the robot from model inference, SLAM/perception, and more gets data, gets scheduled and runs efficiently on shared compute. You’ll work alongside teammates who own the runtime and our build and delivery infrastructure, and you’ll partner cross-functionally with ML, SLAM/Perception, Controls and Hardware teams to ensure the GPU is a first-class, well-utilized resource that meets the latency and throughput requirements of a real-time robotic system operating in the home.
What You’ll Do
You’ll own and contribute to the accelerated compute layer of the ML & Robotics Infra, including:
• Efficient model execution and switching: Reduce gpu kernel launch overheads and make swapping between models on the same device fast and predictable
• GPU scheduling and time-slicing: Arbitrate GPU access across concurrent users (model inference, SLAM, and other robotics applications) with predictable latency
• Camera pipeline: Drive low-latency transfer of camera frames into GPU memory, integrating with HW accelerate encode/decode (NVDEC/NVENC) where appropriate
• CPU ↔ GPU data transfer: Build efficient, low-overhead data movement between host and device, including pinned memory, zero-copy paths, and asynchronous transfer patterns
• CPU/GPU synchronization: Design synchronization primitives and patterns that minimize stalls and keep inference pipelines full
What You’ll Bring
• 2+ years of experience developing gpu systems software
• Strong proficiency in CUDA and a systems language such as C++, C, or Rust
• Solid understanding of GPU architecture, GPU workloads, and the tradeoffs involved in time-slicing and sharing the device across users
• Hands-on experience with the CUDA ecosystem: CUDA runtime API, CUDA Graphs, and CUDA IPC
• Familiarity with GPU sharing mechanisms such as MPS and MIG
• Experience with GPU profiling tools such as Nsight Systems and Nsight Compute
• Solid Linux fundamentals: scheduling, IPC, memory management, and performance tuning
Nice to Have
• Contributions to CUDA libraries or other GPU programming libraries
• Experience with camera pipeline integration and NVDEC/NVENC
• Experience optimizing model inference on embedded GPU platforms (e.g., Jetson)
• Experience with observability and tracing for GPU-accelerated workloads
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: Systems Software Engineer
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
The ML & Robotics Infra team builds the foundational systems that every part of our robot perception, ML, controls and behavior runs on, and the developer infrastructure that lets us build, ship, and update that software quickly and safely on every robot in the fleet.
As a System Software Engineer, you’ll focus on the runtime itself which encompasses the IPC layer, performance, scheduling, observability, logging and replay infrastructure that the rest of the robot software depends on. You’ll work alongside teammates who own the GPU and accelerated compute layer and our build and delivery infrastructure, and you’ll partner cross-functionally with Software, ML, Controls, and Hardware teams to ensure the runtime meets the latency, throughput, and reliability bar required for a real-time robotic system operating in the home.
What You’ll Do
You’ll own and contribute to core areas of the runtime, including:
• IPC: Build the messaging substrate for high-throughput sensor data, messaging and low-latency event flow between processes
• Data logging & dynamic trigger infrastructure: Develop a high-throughput logging system and (in the future) trigger-based capture for targeted debugging in the field
• CPU performance tuning & code optimization: Profile, identify bottlenecks, and drive down latency across the stack
• Scheduling and monitoring: Design how work gets dispatched, prioritized, and monitored on the robot
• Observability & tracing: Build tracing, profiling, and runtime introspection so engineers across the company can understand what the robot is doing and why
• Offline replay: Enable faithful replay of recorded runs for debugging, regression testing, and ML iteration
What you’ll bring
• 2+ years of experience developing systems software for high-performance, low-latency, or pseudo-realtime environments (robotics, autonomous vehicles, HFT, or similar)
• Strong proficiency in a systems language such as C++, C, or Rust
• Deep Linux expertise: scheduling, IPC, kernel tuning, memory management, and performance tuning
• Hands-on experience with CPU performance optimization
• Strong systems debugging skills with tools like perf, ftrace, eBPF, and similar tracing infrastructure
Nice to have
• Experience writing systems software for AV, Robotics or other physical systems
• Experience building observability or tracing infrastructure for distributed or multi-process systems
• Experience designing or building high-throughput logging systems and exposure deterministic replay systems
• Experience with build systems and OTA updates
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: Recruiting Coordinator
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
We are looking for a proactive, detail-oriented Recruiting Coordinator to boost our hiring engine by managing interviews and supporting the talent team. This role is perfect for someone dedicated to providing an exceptional candidate experience in a fast-paced environment.
This role requires more than passive support. We want someone who takes true ownership, acts with urgency, and possesses a genuine commitment to precision. If you are motivated by organized processes, can maintain focus under pressure, and are eager to advance your career in talent acquisition, we invite you to apply.
Location: Onsite 5 days per week in our Redwood City office
Compensation: $40-45/hour. This is a contract role and does not include company benefits.
What You'll Do
• Manage end-to-end interview scheduling across multiple roles, formats, and time zones, keeping every moving part organized and on time
• Work closely with recruiters and hiring managers to troubleshoot conflicts, align on timelines, and keep hiring on track
• Own the accuracy and cleanliness of candidate records and pipeline data in Ashby
• Support recruiters and HR partners with operational tasks that keep hiring moving efficiently
• Look for ways to make the process better: if something is slow, unclear, or broken, raise it and help fix it
What You'll Bring
• 1–3 years of experience in recruiting coordination, HR, or a similar operations-focused role, ideally in a startup or high-growth environment
• Required: hands-on experience with Ashby ATS
• Strong organizational skills and the ability to juggle multiple open roles simultaneously without losing focus or quality
• Clear, professional communication: candidates and teammates always know where things stand
• Sharp attention to detail, especially when it comes to data: you catch mistakes before they cascade
• Comfortable working independently, reprioritizing quickly, and staying composed when things get busy
• Sound judgment when handling sensitive or confidential information
Nice to Have
• Experience coordinating interviews for technical or high-volume roles
• Familiarity with HRIS platforms such as Rippling, Workday, or similar
• Prior experience with background check platforms (e.g., Checkr or Sterling)
• Exposure to employer branding or recruiting event coordination
Benefits
• Free lunch and dinner provided
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

title: 3D Character Animator (6 month contract)
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
We are seeking an experienced 3D Character Animator (6 month contract) to join our product team. In this role, you will create iconic character moments and subtle physical cues for human-robot interaction that make Memo a pleasure to interact with. This contract has the potential to transition into a full-time role based on performance and business needs.
What You'll Do
• Craft Iconic Moments: As customer’s first home robot, everything Memo does will make an impression. You’ll shape key moments that establish Memo’s approachability and reliability.
• Rapid Iteration with Engineering and Product Teams: As part of a cross-functional team, you’ll prepare several rough mock ups for each interaction, then refine and down-select them iteratively by incorporating feedback from product, engineering and users.
• Build Memo’s Movement Vocabulary: Define and refine Memo’s movement style as it goes about household tasks.
What You'll Bring
• Industry-Grade Animation Experience: A portfolio demonstrating 3D character work in industry-standard tools such as Maya or Blender.
• Movement Design for Physical Products: Experience in robotics, installation art, or related fields creating interactive experiences in physical space.
• Excellent Communication, Collaboration and Documentation: A strong commitment to clarifying constraints and opportunities, tracking animation versions, and synthesizing feedback to guide future animations.
• An Explorer’s Spirit: We’re charting new territory. If harnessing mid-process discoveries and overcoming unexpected obstacles to bring a character to life excites you, this is the job for you!
Nice To Haves
• Rigging or Modeling Experience: Experience building your own rigs or 3D environments to place characters in a broader world.
• Procedural or Responsive Animation Experience: Past work with animations that respond to user inputs or dynamic environments.
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

title: Design Engineer
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
We’re looking for a Design Engineer to own the design and development of Memo’s companion app. This role sits at the intersection of product design, prototyping and software development. You’ll rapidly explore interactions, build working prototypes, and collaborate closely with product, software and hardware teams to turn complex system capabilities into simple, intuitive experiences.
This role is especially important during our upcoming beta: The app will be how families set up, understand and command their robot. You will define how people interact with robots in a world where robots are everywhere.
What You'll Do
• Design the Memo companion app experience: Define the interaction for how users prepare their homes and use Memo on a daily basis.
• Prototype interaction concepts quickly: Build high-fidelity prototypes and interactive demos.
• Develop production features: Implement core parts of the companion app so prototypes can evolve directly into real product experiences.
• Collaborate across disciplines: Partner closely with product, software, hardware and operations to identify requirements and integrate them elegantly into the user experience.
What You'll Bring
• A strong eye for aesthetics and flexibility to expand beyond the established design language
• Ability to prototype ideas quickly and translate them into working software
• Experience collaborating closely with engineers and cross-functional teams
• Comfort working in early-stage product environments where requirements evolve quickly
• A bias toward building and testing ideas
Nice to Have
• Experience designing interfaces for physical devices or hardware products
• Experience working on early-stage products (0→1 development)
• Experience working with voice interactions and other human-robot interactions
• Strong interest in robotics or smart home products and systems
• Strong prototyping skills using tools such as React Native or similar frameworks
What You're Not
• A designer who prefers static mockups over interactive prototypes
• Looking for a role where requirements are fully defined before work begins
• Most effective when working in narrow design scopes rather than shaping the full product experience
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

title: Robotics Systems Engineer
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to ExpectIn building robots for the home, we are tackling a true grand challenge in robotics: dexterous and safe mobile manipulation in unstructured environments. To make this possible, we are building across the entire robotics stack. We’re training state-of-the-art AI models that leverage our large-scale, high-quality, real-world data collection system. At the same time, we’re building a new kind of consumer hardware product, which will be deployed into homes and delivering real value for real customers. As an early member of a small, cross-functional team, you’ll play a key role in pushing both our technology and product forward: advancing the frontier of embodied AI, and soon giving countless hours back to our customers so they can spend more time on the things they value most.
As a Robotics System Integration Engineer, you’ll play a central role in delivering reliable, safe, and capable home robots to consumers at scale. You’ll work closely with mechanical, electrical, and software engineers to diagnose and resolve issues with our existing robots. You’ll use these findings to refine our manufacturing and QA processes, and inform the system architecture and requirements for future design cycles. This role offers significant scope and ownership, and the chance to help shape cutting-edge technology into a cohesive, trustworthy product.
What You’ll Do• Integrate and test robotic systems, including both actuation and perception hardware
• Triage and debug cross-disciplinary issues spanning mechanical, electrical, firmware, and software systems
• Bring up, test, and evaluate key components, such as sensors and actuators
• Maintain motor and sensor drivers, and related software tools, in Python / C++
• Develop clear processes for robot assembly, bringup, QA testing, and troubleshooting
• Help define component and subsystem performance requirements, and develop test plans to ensure reliable robot operation across a diverse range of tasks and environments
• Support design of robot HW and SW systems, collaborating across teams to optimize the architecture of next-gen robot products for safety, performance, reliability, cost, and manufacturability
What You’ll Bring• 3+ years of experience working with complex robotic or electromechanical systems
• Proficiency in Python or C++
• Strong system-level understanding of robotics hardware, firmware, and software layers
• Deep familiarity with standard diagnostic tools (oscilloscope, multimeter, logic analyzers)
• Experience developing test plans and procedures for electromechanical systems
Nice to haves• Deep experience in protocols including CAN, Ethernet, USB, MIPI, GMSL
• Experience with quasi-direct-drive actuators
• Experience calibrating computer vision systems
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: SLAM Engineer
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to ExpectIn building robots for the home, we are tackling a true grand challenge in robotics: dexterous and safe mobile manipulation in unstructured environments. To make this possible, we are building across the entire robotics stack. We’re training state-of-the-art AI models that leverage our large-scale, high-quality, real-world data collection system. At the same time, we’re building a new kind of consumer hardware product, which will be deployed into homes and delivering real value for real customers. As an early member of a small, cross-functional team, you’ll play a key role in pushing both our technology and product forward: advancing the frontier of embodied AI, and soon giving countless hours back to our customers so they can spend more time on the things they value most.
As a SLAM Engineer, your work will be essential in enabling our home robot to navigate safely in complex and dynamic home environments. You’ll work closely with perception, motion planning, and controls engineers to ensure that we leverage our robot’s onboard sensor suite to reliably generate safe, smooth, and natural motions.
What You’ll Do• Build robust indoor SLAM system fusing LiDAR, Camera, IMU and odometry data
• Develop software pipelines and processes for multi-sensor calibration
• Collaborate with perception and motion planning engineers on indoor navigation and collision avoidance
What You’ll Bring• Experience with vision-only or lidar-based SLAM pipelines
• Deep understanding of spatial math including 3D rotation representations, frame transformations, camera projections
• Familiarity with multi-sensor time-synchronization methods
• Experience writing production-quality software in Python or C++
Nice to Have• Experience building production-grade SLAM systems in autonomous driving, AR/VR or robotics industries
• Familiarity with GTSAM
• Experience with motion-capture systems
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. title: Full Stack Software Engineer (Generalist)
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
To architect the next generation of robotic intelligence, we need versatile engineers to craft the systems that make it possible. As a Full Stack Generalist, you will move across our entire ecosystem, from our global data collection pipeline to our ML training and robot management platforms. We are looking for "0 to 1" builders who thrive on solving complex, high-impact challenges rather than sticking to a narrow roadmap.
What You’ll Do
Depending on your strengths and the team’s needs, you could dive into one of several core software pillars:
• Data Operation Tooling: Scaling our "Data Engine" to manage thousands of global collectors and building high-fidelity tools to audit and distill massive amounts of human motion data.
• Machine Learning Systems: Building the "Laboratory" where our ML team evaluates models, including "human-in-the-loop" observability interfaces and large-scale data processing.
• Robot Fleet Management: Architecting our "Mission Control" to securely identify, monitor, and manage our fleet of robots as they deploy into real-world environments.
• Core Infrastructure: Proactively identifying friction across our stack and building the shared services, APIs, and internal tools that allow the entire team to move 10x faster.
What You’ll Bring
• Full-Stack Proficiency: 5+ years of building and scaling cloud-native applications with mastery of modern frontend (e.g., React, TypeScript) and robust backend (e.g., Node.js, Python) stacks.
• Product Intuition: You don't just "take tickets." You talk to stakeholders, understand the underlying problem, and architect a solution that balances speed with long-term stability.
• Systems Fluency: You have a high "rate of learning" and thrive in high-growth environments where shipping is about balancing fast iteration with high-quality engineering across various languages, frameworks, and infrastructure layers.
Nice to have
• Experience as a founding or early hire; able to define roadmaps where no blueprint exists.
• A curiosity for robotics, ML, or IoT, excited to learn how your software interacts with the physical world.
• Experience building platforms that handle terabytes of data or thousands of concurrent devices/users.
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
Even if you don’t meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply. Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back unless they meet 100% of the criteria — we don’t want that to be the reason we miss out on great talent.

title: Full Stack Software Engineer, Data Operations Tooling
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
Data is our lifeblood. We use AR headsets and Skill Capture Gloves™ to capture human motion at scale, and your role as a Full Stack Engineer for Data Operations is to grow the platform that manages this pipeline, from tracking thousands of memory developers across the globe to building the high-fidelity tools used to audit, filter, and qualify hundreds of hours of data per day.
What You’ll Do
• Memory Developer Systems: Partner with the Data Operations team to build an internal platform that manages thousands of remote memory developers, tracking performance, monitoring hardware health, etc., to ensure a steady, high-quality data stream.
• The Audit Lab: Develop high-performance React interfaces for replaying and verifying human motion data, enabling our team to curate "gold-standard" datasets, from thousands of uploads.
• Data Ingestion & Orchestration: Architect robust backend services to handle massive amounts of data, ensuring it is ready for audit.
• Startup Fluidity: While this role focuses on Data Ops, we operate in a high-growth environment. You should be comfortable with shifting priorities and jumping into other parts of the stack as we scale.
What You’ll Bring
• Full-Stack Proficiency: 3+ years of building and scaling cloud-native applications with mastery of modern frontend (e.g., React, TypeScript) and robust backend (e.g., Node.js, Python) stacks.
• Operational Mindset: Experience building high-throughput internal tooling or "human-in-the-loop" platforms.
• UX for Experts: A high bar for building low-friction, data-heavy interfaces.
Nice to have
• Experience as a founding or early hire; able to define roadmaps where no blueprint exists.
• Familiarity with synchronized playback.
• Experience with marketplace, gig-work, or data-labeling platforms.

title: Full Stack Software Engineer, ML Data & Evaluations
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.


## What to Expect
You are the bridge between raw data and robotic intelligence. As a Full Stack Engineer, ML Data & Evals, you will build the "Laboratory" where our ML team evaluates and deploys models. Your work accelerates the research-to-production loop, creating the infrastructure to launch on-robot evaluations and visualize model performance in complex, real-world scenarios.


## What You’ll Do
• Interactive Data Systems: Architect the interfaces and engines that unify robot and Skill Capture Gloves™ data, enabling "human-in-the-loop" workflows, from episode annotation to seamless switching between autonomous execution and manual teleoperation intervention.
• Evaluation & Benchmarking: Develop high-performance tools to compare model-driven motion against human-captured ground truth, helping quantify model progress across diverse tasks.
• Data Processing & Orchestration: Architect processing services that transform raw captures and sensor data into ML-ready formats, ensuring a seamless flow from our global collection systems to the models that power our robots.
• Startup Fluidity: While this role focuses on the ML Platform, our Full Stack Engineers are comfortable shifting priorities and domains, eager to jump into other parts of the stack as we scale.


## What You’ll Bring
• Full-Stack Proficiency: 3+ years of building and scaling cloud-native applications with mastery of modern frontend (e.g., React, TypeScript) and robust backend (e.g., Node.js, Python) stacks.
• Operational Mindset: Experience building high-throughput internal tooling or "human-in-the-loop" platforms.
• Interactive ML Observability: A high bar for building low-friction interfaces that make complex model behaviors, sensor data, and "human-in-the-loop" interventions easy to interpret and act upon.


## Nice to Have
• Experience as a founding or early hire; able to define roadmaps where no blueprint exists.
• Experience building robust ETL pipelines that transform terabytes of multi-modal data into structured, high-quality datasets.
• Familiarity with tools like Weights & Biases, MLFlow, or similar experiment tracking frameworks.

title: Full Stack Software Engineer, Robot Fleet Management
description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Home Robotics
At Sunday, we're developing personal robots to reclaim the hours lost to repetitive tasks. We're focused on an ambitious goal to make generalized robots broadly accessible, enabling households to take back quality time.
We have spent the last 18 months building a talented team, securing capital, and validating our technology. We are now seeking passionate individuals to join us in the next phase of our growth. If you are ready to apply your skills to the forefront of robotics innovation, we’d love to hear from you.
What to Expect
As a Full Stack Engineer for Robot Management, you are responsible for the "Mission Control" of our fleet. You will architect the infrastructure that allows us to securely identify, access, and manage our robots at scale. Because our robots operate in private homes, your work sits at the critical intersection of high-availability systems, rigorous security, and user privacy.
What You’ll Do
• Fleet Identity & Security: Design and implement robust robot authentication and registration systems, ensuring secure "handshakes" between our cloud and hardware in the field.
• Observability & Health: Build the centralized telemetry and alerting systems that monitor robot availability, hardware health, and system performance across the global fleet.
• Privacy-First Infrastructure: Develop the systems that manage data privacy and residency, ensuring that as we deploy into homes, our fleet remains a trusted and secure presence.
• Startup Fluidity: While this role focuses on Robot Management, we operate in a high-growth environment. You should be comfortable with shifting priorities and jumping into other parts of the stack as we scale.
What You’ll Bring
• Full-Stack Proficiency: 3+ years of building and scaling end-to-end applications with mastery of robust backend architectures (e.g., Python, C++), and ability to deliver clean functional frontend interfaces (e.g., React, TypeScript).
• Reliability Mindset: Experience building systems where "downtime" or security vulnerabilities have real-world physical consequences.
• IoT/Edge Intuition: A strong understanding of secure remote access, device state synchronization, and handling intermittent connectivity.
Nice to have
• Experience as a founding or early hire; able to define roadmaps where no blueprint exists.
• Experience with Zero Trust architectures, PKI, or managing device-level certificates at scale.
• Familiarity with large-scale IoT fleets, robotics middleware and over-the-air update delivery systems.
• Proficiency with Docker and containerization strategies for deploying and managing edge services.
At Sunday Robotics, we’re building technology shaped by real people — curious, creative, and diverse. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.
